title: hieracium pilosella subsp. sedunorum
intro: Hieracium pilosella subsp. sedunorum, also known as creeping hawkweed, is a subspecies of the hawkweed plant native to Europe, specifically found in the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, with medicinal compounds that include flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and acting as an antioxidant. Traditionally, creeping hawkweed is used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
